author: y4rr
content: Cascade discovered by Phase 2.14 plumbing conformance suite. Test case: `cast::<f64, bf16>(1e300_f64)` is expected to return Err(InvalidArgument) per the cast contract ("value not representable"). Actual: returns Ok(bf16::INFINITY) because num_traits::NumCast for half::bf16 silently saturates to Infinity instead of returning None. The same call for f32 → bf16 with a value > bf16::MAX would saturate identically. Two possible fixes: (a) wrap NumCast<bf16> with an explicit finite-result check that errors on Infinity that wasn't already infinite in the source; (b) document this as a divergence from cast::<T,i32>(Infinity) semantics. The phase 2.14 test suite skips this case (huge → bf16) and only asserts the other expect_err cases until this is resolved.
